title: Dispatcher: total dispatch attempt ceiling to prevent infinite retry spirals
description:
The dispatcher retries up to `DEFAULT_MAX_ATTEMPTS=3` per dispatcher run. But when a task's session dies and the stale-claim release fires, the retry counter resets on the next dispatch. An agent can effectively be dispatched indefinitely on a permanently broken task.

Fix: add a `dispatch.total_attempts` counter to the runtime state that accumulates across claim releases and is never reset. When `total_attempts >= MAX_TOTAL_ATTEMPTS` (suggest 9 = 3 runs × 3 attempts), block the task permanently with `failure_reason: 'Exceeded maximum total dispatch attempts (9). Manual intervention required.'`

Changes:
1. In `buildRuntimeTaskState()`: include `total_attempts: task.dispatch?.total_attempts || 0`
2. In the dispatch loop: increment `task.dispatch.total_attempts` on each attempt (not just each run)
3. Before dispatching: check `task.dispatch?.total_attempts >= MAX_TOTAL_ATTEMPTS` and block if exceeded
4. Add `ROOK_DISPATCH_MAX_TOTAL_ATTEMPTS` env var (default 9) to service file

Do NOT reset `total_attempts` on stale claim release. Only reset when status transitions to `done`.
